topic: vLLM vs TGI vs Triton for LLM serving

messages.content: Need to self-host Llama 3.3 70B for our product. What inference server: vLLM, TGI (Text Generation Inference), or NVIDIA Triton?

All three are production-grade. Differences:

**vLLM**:
- PagedAttention — best memory efficiency for batch serving
- Highest throughput for concurrent users
- Simple API (OpenAI-compatible)
- Speculative decoding support
- Most popular for LLM-specific serving

**TGI** (Hugging Face):
- Production-tested at Hugging Face scale
- Flash Attention, quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF)
- Streaming support, structured output
- Docker-first deployment
- Good for HF model hub integration

**NVIDIA Triton**:
- General purpose (not LLM-specific)
- TensorRT-LLM backend for NVIDIA GPUs
- Multi-model serving, dynamic batching
- Best for mixed workloads (LLM + CV + embeddings)
- Most complex to configure

For serving just Llama 70B: **vLLM**. For multi-model serving on NVIDIA GPUs: **Triton + TensorRT-LLM**.
We have 4x A100 80GB. Can vLLM do tensor parallelism across them?

Yes, vLLM supports tensor parallelism out of the box:

```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct \
  --tensor-parallel-size 4 \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 \
  --max-model-len 8192
```

4x A100 80GB = 320GB VRAM total. Llama 70B in FP16 needs ~140GB, so you have headroom for:
- Larger context windows (up to 128k with proper rope scaling)
- KV cache for many concurrent users
- Or run quantized (AWQ/GPTQ) and serve on 2 GPUs, use other 2 for a separate model

Expected throughput with 4x A100: ~2000-4000 tokens/sec for batch inference, depending on batch size and sequence length.

vLLM's OpenAI-compatible API means your application code uses the standard OpenAI SDK — just point it at your vLLM server URL.
